macro_summary: Tuesday's session saw tech and semis sell off sharply (SOXX -4.2%, Nasdaq -1.5%) as oil prices surged 4.7% to $72.17 on renewed Iran-US tensions (IRGC fired on commercial ships, US airstrikes overnight, per crypto briefings and logistics reports). Oil's spike revived inflation nerves, pushing bond yields higher (10Y to 4.53%), while the China ag tariff rollback provided a modest positive offset. The chip rout was compounded by Samsung's profit beat failing to satisfy lofty AI expectations (per CNBC).
commodities_note: WTI surged 4.7% to $72.17 after Iran fired missiles at ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz, reversing some of the prior month's -22% decline. Gold slipped 0.3% to $4,130.6 as yields ticked higher. DXY edged up 0.2% to 101.12.

singapore.summary: All three Singapore banks remain hard-blocked on extreme extension (RSI 77-84, 5-12% above 50-day), with NIM relief stalled as US10Y persists above 4.20% and MAS policy unchanged. OCBC has outperformed on YoY profit growth but the extension block is absolute across the cluster — no entry until a meaningful pullback resets technicals.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally impaired — NIM compression from dual channels persists
singapore.key_development: Oil spike and renewed geopolitical risk push yields higher, reinforcing the USD funding cost headwind for SGD-denominated lending.

semiconductors.summary: SOXX tumbled 4.2% as Samsung's blowout profit failed to satisfy AI expectations, triggering a broad semiconductor selloff. NVDA tested the 200-day SMA, while ASML, MU, and AMD hold above their 50-day but remain extended. The cluster is structurally bullish on AI demand but tactically broken — only three WATCH signals and zero BUY/ACCUMULATE names highlight the lack of actionable entries.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically extended — earnings bar set too high for current price levels
semiconductors.key_development: DeepSeek's custom chip development (per Reuters) and Samsung's disappointment add competitive overhangs, while the Iran-driven oil spike pressures risk appetite.

big_tech.summary: MSFT upgraded to WATCH on a favourable R:R near the 50-day, while AMZN and GOOG remain HOLD with uninteresting risk/reward. The cluster is diverging: MSFT's non-OpenAI RPO growth is a cleaner demand signal, while AMZN's bond sale and Meta's cloud entry raise competitive concerns.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — capex confirmed, but MSFT's OpenAI dependency and AMZN's margin uncertainty weigh
big_tech.key_development: MSFT's legal overhang (Copilot/data center noise lawsuits) adds noise, but the $25B AMZN bond sale and Meta's AI cloud push are the structural drivers this week.

interconnected.name: Samsung Electronics
interconnected.reason: Third HBM3E supplier; qualification status with NVDA directly affects memory supply diversity for NVDA's Blackwell ramp. Samsung's earnings miss on high expectations (per CNBC, Jul 7) chilled memory stocks including MU and SK Hynix.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if Samsung confirms HBM3E qualification for NVDA's next-gen GPUs, or if memory ASPs show a sustained recovery above current cycle levels.

interconnected.name: Coherent
interconnected.reason: NVDA's $2B photonics investment partner alongside LITE (per NVIDIA IR, Mar 2 2026). Optical interconnects are the bandwidth bottleneck for GPU clusters, making COHR a direct read-through on NVDA's AI infrastructure buildout.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if LITE's Greensboro fab buildout triggers a co-investor re-rate or if Coherent reports accelerating AI optics orders above $500M quarterly.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: AI datacenter networking upgrades benefit Arista — NOK's AI networking pivot and NVDA's Spectrum-X adoption are the shared tailwind. Watch for hyperscaler switch-spend commentary in MSFT/AMZN earnings.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if MSFT's July 29 earnings show Azure networking capex above $50B annualized, directly lifting Arista's addressable market.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC designer competing with AVGO on data-center silicon, benefiting from the same hyperscaler shift to in-house chips. MRVL's Amazon Trainium win and Google TPU adjacency link it directly to AMZN and GOOG capex.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if GOOG's July 23 TPU ramp guidance confirms a second-source ASIC supplier (MRVL) or if AVGO signals a crowded custom-silicon pipeline.

active_risks: Iran fired missiles at commercial ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz, and US forces retaliated with strikes late Tuesday — the ceasefire has collapsed, threatening oil supply routes.
China is considering restricting overseas access to its most advanced AI models (per Reuters, Jul 7), potentially deepening the US-China technology rift and affecting Chinese AI-exposed semis.
NY Fed survey shows rising near-term inflation expectations (3.7% vs 3.5%), while oil's surge could reignite Fed rate-hike bets ahead of next week's CPI report.
Samsung's blowout earnings failed to meet elevated AI expectations, triggering a global semiconductor selloff and raising the bar for AI-linked earnings across the sector.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil's spike to $72.17 reversed a month of energy-cost relief, pushing 10Y yields to 4.53% and reviving inflation fears. Semis sold off hard (SOXX -4.2%) as the combination of AI-demand doubts, DeepSeek's custom chip, and geopolitical premium created a risk-off impulse. NVDA and TSM held near supports, while the Singapore banks remained extended on the rate-haven bid.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Iran's IRGC directly fired missiles at two commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz (per Axios and Cryptobriefing, Jul 7), with US forces conducting retaliatory strikes late Tuesday. China announced it will drop its 10% tariff on US agricultural goods, signalling a potential de-escalation on the trade front.

base.description: Hormuz tensions persist but oil remains below $80 as US-Iran diplomacy re-engages after the strikes; WTI settles near $72, US10Y holds at 4.5%. Semis continue to consolidate with no fresh catalyst — NVDA and TSM hold supports, while ASML's July 15 earnings provide the next binary. Singapore banks stay elevated on rate-haven flows, extension blocks remain.

optimistic.description: US-Iran ceasefire talks resume quickly, oil drops back to $68-70, and the CPI report next week shows cooling inflation, prompting the Fed to signal a pause. Semis rally 5-7% in relief; NVDA reclaims the 50-day, ASML breaks out post-earnings, and the Singapore banks finally pull back to actionable levels.

pessimistic.description: Iran-U.S. escalation widens — Hormuz transits stall completely, oil spikes above $85, and WTI surge pushes 10Y above 4.8%. Semis sell off another 5-10% as AI demand fears compound oil shock: NVDA loses the 200-day, TSM breaks $400, and the Singapore banks crash toward their 50-day averages, overshooting to the downside.

wildcard.description: DeepSeek's custom AI chip (per Reuters) reaches mass production faster than expected, challenging NVDA's merchant GPU model. Combined with China's AI model export restrictions, this triggers a tech valuation reset: NVDA drops 15%, AVGO loses its custom-ASIC premium, while PLTR's defense AI narrative rides the geopolitical chaos to a new high.

geopolitical.portfolio_action: Maintain a defensive posture — no new entries while VIX remains elevated and semis sell off. Wait for the CPI print on July 14 and ASML earnings on July 15 to provide clarity before deploying fresh capital. Stagger any potential entries after binary events, starting with half-positions on WATCH names like NVDA and MSFT only if technicals confirm.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Defensive — semis under pressure, oil spike rekindles inflation fears, and extension blocks limit entry opportunities
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 4 of 29 names are hard-blocked on technical extension (D05, O39, U11, PLTR). The semis rout deepens caution across the watchlist, with only three WATCH signals amid broad HOLD and CAUTION.

new_stocks_to_watch.name: Samsung Electronics
new_stocks_to_watch.reason: Memory and foundry giant directly read-through on MU and SK Hynix HBM demand. Samsung's HBM3E qualification with NVDA would break the memory duopoly, making it a key signpost.
new_stocks_to_watch.entry_note: Add to watchlist if Samsung confirms NVDA HBM3E qualification on its next earnings call or if memory ASPs show a durable recovery.

new_stocks_to_watch.name: Coherent
new_stocks_to_watch.reason: NVDA's $2B photonics partner alongside LITE; optical interconnects are the bandwidth bottleneck for AI clusters. COHR is the direct LITE peer in the NVDA photonics ecosystem.
new_stocks_to_watch.entry_note: Add to watchlist if Coherent reports AI optics orders accelerating above $500M quarterly or if NVDA announces additional photonics partnerships.

new_stocks_to_watch.name: Marvell Technology
new_stocks_to_watch.reason: Custom ASIC designer benefiting from hyperscaler shift to in-house silicon; Amazon Trainium and potential Google TPU adjacency tie it directly to AMZN/GOOG capex.
new_stocks_to_watch.entry_note: Add to watchlist if GOOG's July 23 earnings confirm a second-source ASIC supplier or if AVGO signals a near-capacity custom-silicon pipeline.

macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: Headline CPI drops below 4.0% and core CPI shows easing, reinforcing the Fed pause narrative. Oil's recent spike not yet in the data, allowing a relief rally.
bullish_upgrades: NVDA → ACCUMULATE candidate (inflation pivot boosts semis)
MU → WATCH (memory cycle thesis gets macro tailwind)
ASML → WATCH (if CPI cool precedes strong earnings)
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: CPI accelerates to 4.5%+ with rising shelter costs, reigniting rate-hike bets. Combined with oil spike, this would crush growth stocks.
bearish_impact: NVDA → remains WATCH with downward bias
ASML → deepened CAUTION on rate sensitivity
MSFT → returns to HOLD as tech multiples compress

macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: ASML beats on bookings and guides 2nm EUV tool shipments above consensus, with high-NA adoption accelerating. China export risk remains contained.
bullish_upgrades: ASML → WATCH (strong bookings justify premium) if post-earnings price breaks above $1,800
TSM → WATCH (read-across on EUV demand) if ASML order book impresses
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: Bookings miss and China revenue exposure becomes a flashpoint as MATCH Act commentary resurfaces. Guidance cut on memory headwinds.
bearish_impact: ASML → deepened CAUTION (re-rating risk on semi capex cycle)
TSM → returns to CAUTION (ASML miss signals softer demand)
NVDA → remains WATCH but with lower conviction

calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop. Treat as a reader-facing self-knowledge summary.
